sourceLabel: Mental Availability source name, wave, and owner

topline.label: Measured CEP read headline
topline.read: Plain-English summary of what the source says about mental availability and category entry points.
topline.strategicQuestion: The most important strategic question this source should help answer.

mental_penetration.read: How many category buyers link the brand to at least one relevant CEP.

mental_market_share.read: The brand's share of measured CEP associations in the competitive mental set.

network_size.read: The number and breadth of CEPs where the brand has usable memory links.

share_of_mind.read: The brand's relative accessibility across measured CEPs.

ceps.name: Better snack for my family
ceps.consumerQuestion: What can I feel good about putting out for my family?
ceps.interpretation: Measured source supports building this buying situation first.
ceps.action: Build with consistent family-snacking cues and retail proof.
evidence: Brand association exceeds the team's threshold.
ceps.caveat: Measured source still needs stakeholder methodology review before final decisioning.

ceps.name: Better-for-you party snack
ceps.consumerQuestion: What snack feels fun and a little better for a party?
ceps.interpretation: Attractive situation, but competitive pressure is high.
ceps.action: Watch and test before scaling investment.
ceps.caveat: This is not proof of occasion ownership, substitution, or cannibalization.
